hooks.command: REPOMIX_OUT="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/repomix-output.xml"; cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"; python3 -c "import os,time,sys; f=sys.argv[1]; exit(0 if os.path.exists(f) and time.time()-os.path.getmtime(f)<86400 else 1)" "$REPOMIX_OUT" 2>/dev/null || npx repomix --config repomix.config.json 2>/dev/null || true; STATS=$(python3 -c "import os,sys; f=sys.argv[1]; s=os.path.getsize(f)//1024 if os.path.exists(f) else 0; c=open(f).read().count('<file ') if s else 0; print(f'{c} files, {s}KB') if s else print('')" "$REPOMIX_OUT" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); [ -n "$STATS" ] && echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"Repomix snapshot ready ($STATS). Attach .claude/repomix-output.xml to any subagent for full codebase context.\"}" || true

SESSION_FILE="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/sessions/last-session.json"; [ -f "$SESSION_FILE" ] && F="$SESSION_FILE" python3 -c "import json,os,time; f=os.environ['F']; d=json.load(open(f)); age=int((time.time()-os.path.getmtime(f))/60); b=d.get('branch','?'); uc=d.get('uncommitted_count',0); rc=d.get('recent_commits',''); prs=d.get('open_prs',''); parts=['branch='+b]; uc and parts.append(str(uc)+' uncommitted file(s)'); rc and parts.append('commits: '+rc.replace('|',', ')); prs and parts.append('open PRs: '+prs); print(json.dumps({'systemMessage':'Last session '+str(age)+'min ago — '+', '.join(parts)+'.'}))" 2>/dev/null || true

mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/sessions" && cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo unknown) && DIRTY_COUNT=$(git status --short 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ' || echo 0) && DIRTY_FILES=$(git status --short 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | head -20 | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//') && RECENT=$(git log --oneline -3 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' '|' | sed 's/|$//') && OPEN_PRS=$(gh pr list --state open --json number,title --jq '[.[0:5][] | "#" + (.number|tostring) + ": " + .title] | join(", ")' 2>/dev/null || echo '') && BRANCH="$BRANCH" DIRTY="$DIRTY_COUNT" FILES="$DIRTY_FILES" RECENT="$RECENT" PRS="$OPEN_PRS" ENDED="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" python3 -c "import json,os; d={'ended_at':os.environ.get('ENDED',''),'branch':os.environ.get('BRANCH',''),'uncommitted_count':int(os.environ.get('DIRTY','0') or 0),'changed_files':[x for x in os.environ.get('FILES','').split(',') if x],'recent_commits':os.environ.get('RECENT',''),'open_prs':os.environ.get('PRS','')}; print(json.dumps(d))" > "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/sessions/last-session.json" 2>/dev/null || true

cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && git status --short 2>/dev/null | { read -r line; if [ -n "$line" ]; then echo '{"systemMessage": "Warning: uncommitted changes exist. Consider committing before ending the session."}'; fi; } || true

input=$(cat); file=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); case "$file" in *ruff.toml|*tsconfig.json|*.eslintrc*|*tailwind.config.*|*.bandit.toml) echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Config protection: edits to quality gate files (ruff.toml, tsconfig.json, .eslintrc*, tailwind.config.*, .bandit.toml) require explicit user approval. State what you want to change and why before proceeding."}}' ;; *pyproject.toml) changed=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); ti=d.get('tool_input',{}); print(ti.get('old_string','') + ti.get('new_string','') + ti.get('content',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); if echo "$changed" | grep -qE '\[tool\.(ruff|mypy|bandit|pylint)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Config protection: edits to [tool.ruff], [tool.mypy], or [tool.bandit] sections in pyproject.toml require explicit user approval. State what you want to change and why before proceeding."}}'; fi ;; esac || true

hooks.prompt: Check if this file change violates any of these rules: 1) No print() in Python (must use structlog), 2) No console.log in TypeScript (must use proper logger), 3) No 'any' type in TypeScript, 4) No PII/transcripts/API keys in logs or comments, 5) No audio stored to disk. Tool input: $ARGUMENTS. If all rules pass, respond with this exact JSON: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "permissionDecision": "allow"}}. If a rule is violated, respond with: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "permissionDecision": "deny", "permissionDecisionReason": "Describe the specific violation here"}}.

hooks.command: input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo ''); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'origin\s+(main|master)\b|(main|master)\s*$'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Direct push to main/master is blocked. Create a feature branch and open a PR instead."}}'; fi || true

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.py) cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/backend" 2>/dev/null && ruff check --fix "$f" 2>/dev/null && ruff format "$f" 2>/dev/null ;; *.ts|*.tsx) cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/frontend" 2>/dev/null && npx eslint --fix "$f" 2>/dev/null ;; esac; } || true

hooks.statusMessage: Checking if PR needs to be created...

hooks.command: COUNTER_FILE="/tmp/claude_tool_count_${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-$$}"; COUNT=$(cat "$COUNTER_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); COUNT=$((COUNT + 1)); echo "$COUNT" > "$COUNTER_FILE"; if [ $((COUNT % 40)) -eq 0 ]; then echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"Tool call #$COUNT reached. Consider running /compact now to preserve context quality before the next milestone.\"}"; fi || true

BRANCH=$(cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); COUNTER_FILE="/tmp/claude_tool_count_${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-$$}"; COUNT=$(cat "$COUNTER_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo '?'); echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"Compacting at tool call ~$COUNT on branch $BRANCH. After compaction: next migration=019, CAST(:x AS jsonb), user_id on every query, audio never to disk.\"}"

echo '{"systemMessage": "Context compacted. Critical reminders: 1) JSONB mutation: always reassign whole dict. 2) asyncpg: CAST(:param AS jsonb) never ::jsonb. 3) Audio never to disk. 4) Evidence = {start_ms, end_ms} spans. 5) user_id filter on every query. 6) structlog only, never print(). 7) Next migration: 019."}'

BRANCH=$(cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"Subagent context — InterviewCraft: FastAPI+SQLAlchemy backend, Next.js+TypeScript frontend. Rules: user_id on every query, CAST(:x AS jsonb) not ::jsonb, audio never to disk, structlog not print(), no TypeScript any. Next migration: 019. Current branch: $BRANCH.\"}"
echo '{"systemMessage": "Task created. Reminders before starting: next migration is 019, use CAST(:x AS jsonb) not ::jsonb, user_id filter on every query, structlog not print()."}'

cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && git status --short 2>/dev/null | { read -r line; if [ -n "$line" ]; then echo '{"systemMessage": "Task complete — uncommitted changes exist. Commit them before moving to the next task."}'; fi; } || true
hooks.statusMessage: Checking for uncommitted changes after task...

hooks.command: denied=$(cat 2>/dev/null); tool=$(echo "$denied" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(d.get('tool_name','unknown'))" 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"Action blocked by auto mode classifier: $tool. If this is legitimate, explain why and retry — or ask the user to approve it in /permissions.\"}"
